name: Xavier Smith
headline: Explosive Rams gadget receiver is a boom-bust dart
body:
Smith’s 2025 jump came on tiny volume but eye-popping efficiency: a 21.4 percent snap share and 149 routes yielded 24 targets, 18 catches and 303 yards at a sterling 16.83 yards per reception. The 2.03 yards per route run and a 0.914 EPA per play are elite per-route marks, fueled by a deep role—16.88 air yards per target, four catches of 30-plus and two of 40-plus—after just 6 yards in 2024. He still finished only WR92 with 30.3 fantasy points because the target tree is so thin.

That is the whole story: spectacular rates, negligible volume. With a 4.1 percent target share and zero touchdowns despite the big plays, Smith is entirely matchup- and variance-dependent in a crowded Los Angeles receiver room. The deep-ball role gives him real spike-week potential if a target ahead misses time, but the 27-year-old offers no reliable floor. He is a best-ball and deep-league dart only, not a standard redraft starter.
